all_standard.notes: Uses standard agents for all 3 issues. Overpays for NOTIF-001 (trivial) and underpowers NOTIF-003 (complex), risking rework.

tiered_routing.notes: Optimal: fast for NOTIF-001 (5 invocations, $0.01), standard for NOTIF-002 (6 invocations, $0.18), high for NOTIF-003 (6 invocations, $0.90). NOTIF-001 skips subplanner.

standard_with_rework.notes: Standard agents handle NOTIF-003 but need 1.5x cycles due to complexity. Three extra invocations for worker retry and re-testing.

recommendation: Tiered routing saves 31% vs all-standard and 41% vs standard-with-rework. For teams with a typical 60/25/15 trivial/standard/complex mix processing 20+ issues per week, tiered routing provides significant cost savings while ensuring complex tasks get the model capability they need.
breakeven_analysis: Tiered routing breaks even when at least 30% of tasks are trivial. With higher trivial percentages, fast-tier savings accumulate rapidly. The real cost of NOT using high tier for complex tasks is hidden in rework cycles and quality issues.
